Job Title: SAP FI / CO Product Costing Consultant -Australia/New Zealand
Ad Text:
We are HCLTech, one of the fastest-growing large tech companies in the world and home to 219,000+ people across 54 countries, supercharging progress through industry-leading capabilities centered around Digital, Engineering and Cloud.

Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced SAP FI / CO Product Costing Consultant with strong expertise in Product Costing (CO-PC) and S/4HANA Finance. The role focuses on delivering accurate product cost calculations, inventory valuation, and cost transparency to support manufacturing, supply chain, and finance decision‑making.

The ideal candidate will possess deep functional knowledge of standard costing, actual costing, variance analysis, and Material Ledger, and will work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration between Finance, Controlling, and Logistics.

Key Responsibilities
Product Costing (Core Focus)

Configure and support Product Costing (CO-PC) components including:

Cost Component Structures

Costing Variants and Valuation Variants

Quantity structures using BOMs and Routings

Perform Standard Costing (planned cost estimates) and analyze cost updates.

Support Actual Costing using Material Ledger (ML) in S/4HANA.

Configure and support Price Determination and inventory valuation.

Execute and support period-end close activities for product costing and material ledger.

Cost Analysis & Variance Management

Analyze and explain price differences, usage variances, and production variances.

Support WIP calculation, variance calculation, and settlement.

Enable detailed cost transparency for manufacturing and supply chain teams.

Integration & Cross-Functional Collaboration

Ensure seamless integration with:

FI (GL, Inventory Accounting)

PP (Production Planning)

MM (Materials Management)

SD (Sales & Distribution)

Work closely with business stakeholders to understand costing requirements and translate them into SAP solutions.

Reporting & Analytics

Support financial and operational reporting related to:

Product cost build-up

Margin and profitability analysis

Inventory valuation and cost trends

Enable S/4HANA analytics and reporting for cost and profitability insights.

Implementation & Support

Participate in S/4HANA implementations, rollouts, and enhancements.

Support data migration related to costing and material ledger.

Perform unit testing, integration testing, UAT, and hypercare support.

Troubleshoot and resolve production issues in Product Costing and FI/CO areas.

Required Skills & Qualifications
Functional Expertise (Must-Have)

Strong hands-on experience in SAP Product Costing (CO-PC).

In-depth knowledge of:

Material Ledger & Actual Costing (mandatory in S/4HANA)

Cost component structures

BOM & routing-based costing

Variance calculation and settlement

Solid understanding of cost accounting and inventory valuation.

Strong FI–CO integration knowledge.

SAP & Technology Skills

Experience with SAP S/4HANA Finance.

Understanding of manufacturing processes and their impact on costing.

Exposure to CO-PA (preferred but secondary to Product Costing).

Experience with data migration and testing cycles in SAP projects.

Experience

9 to 12+ years of SAP FI/CO experience, with significant focus on Product Costing.

Experience in manufacturing or process industries preferred.

Proven experience in project-based environments and post–go-live support.

Soft Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Ability to explain complex costing concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Certifications (Preferred)

SAP FI or CO Certification.

SAP S/4HANA Finance Certification (nice to have).




Why HCL?

We are one of the fastest-growing large tech companies in the world, with offices in 50+ countries across the globe and 219,000 employees.

Our company is extremely diverse with 165 nationalities represented.

We offer the opportunity to work with colleagues across the globe.

We offer a virtual-first work environment, promoting a good work-life integration and real flexibility.

We offer comprehensive benefits for all employees.

We are a certified great place to work and a top employer in 17 countries, offering a positive work environment that values employee recognition and respect.

How to apply:

To apply for this role, you should have a full work right to work in Australia/ NZ. press "Apply now " button to submit your resume.

HCL's enlightened approach to employee development focus on giving people whatever they need to succeed: be it a virtual assistant or talent transformation sabbaticals; expert guidance or fast track growth; inner peace or democratic empowerment.

Analyse and document current and future state business processes

What You’ll Need   

6+ years’ experience in Business Analysis roles 
Experience across business, digital and/or technology delivery environments 
Exposure to Agile, Waterfall and hybrid delivery methodologies 
Strong facilitation skills with an engaging workshop style 
Proven ability to produce clear, high‑quality requirements and documentation 
Excellent verbal and written communication skills 
Strong analytical skills with the ability to independently solve problems and present recommendations 
Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining accuracy and attention to detail

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we’re happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

What’s Next 

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

The AustralianSuper Story

As a profit-to-member superannuation fund, AustralianSuper is run only for members, and is here to help members achieve their best possible retirement outcome. We don't pay profits or dividends to shareholders, so the money we make goes back into the fund.

We’re an organisation of talented, inspiring colleagues and passionate leaders. As we continue to grow in size and complexity, we need people who will challenge our thinking, deliver outstanding outcomes and most importantly, share in our purpose.

Company description:



DT Infrastructure is a trusted provider of engineering and construction solutions that unlock opportunities for our clients, communities and people. Specialising in Defence, transport and infrastructure, we offer a unique synergy of local expertise in complex projects and the global perspective of our multinational parent company, Gamuda Berhad. Through engineering innovation, collaborative contracting and leading sustainability practices we're unlocking opportunities for a better tomorrow.



Job description:



DT Infrastructure is continuing to strengthen its digital capability across major infrastructure and renewable energy projects, driving the adoption of advanced project systems and data-led delivery.

We are seeking a Senior Project Systems Advisor to act as the Subject Matter Expert for Autodesk Forma, leading the deployment, configuration and optimisation of project systems across projects nationally.

This is a hands-on role suited to someone who has implemented systems end-to-end, not just supported them. You will work closely with project teams to design practical, fit-for-purpose solutions, drive system adoption, and solve complex workflow challenges using tools such as Workato (ACC Connect).

You will support projects across renewable and infrastructure sectors, with occasional travel to site, playing a key role in maturing DT Infrastructure's digital delivery capability and establishing a more integrated, data-driven project environment.

Key Responsibilities
Lead deployment and configuration of Autodesk Forma across projects.
Design and implement system integrations using Workato (ACC Connect).
Provide hands-on support and problem solving for project teams.
Develop training and drive system adoption across national projects.
Build reporting, dashboards and data structures to support delivery.



Profile description:


Proven experience deploying Autodesk Forma on major projects.
Deep functional knowledge of project systems and integrations.
Strong experience with Workato or similar integration platforms.
Hands-on problem solver with ability to design practical solutions.
Experience across infrastructure or renewable project environments.


We offer:


Competitive pay that reflects your contribution: We reward performance and impact, with our remuneration benchmarked to market. 
Purpose-driven careers in infrastructure: As we grow our presence in the renewable energy sector, DTI is also continuing to cement its position as a delivery partner of choice across rail, roads and Defence infrastructure projects. Our teams benefit from the variety of opportunities, applying their skills and experience across multiple sectors. 
Fostering your development: We've developed a culture where continuous learning is front and centre of everything we do. Fostering the growth and development of our people is key to our success and ensuring that we are preparing the next generation of leaders is a core focus for our business.  
Parental leave designed for the reality of family life: Our industry leading parental leave policies are centred on flexibility to support modern family life, because at DTI, little families are a part of our one big family. 
Flexibility that works for you and for us: We acknowledge that everyone's needs are different in striking the right work-life integration. Whether it's hybrid working, adjusted hours, job sharing or balancing family commitments, we'll work together to make it work. 


DT Infrastructure is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace that champions opportunity and respect. Our focus on inclusivity is active and ongoing. Women, people of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent and former members of the Australian Defence Force are encouraged to apply.

Visit www.DTInfrastructure.com.au to explore how DTI can support your career progression, unlock personal advantages and apply for our opportunities.


How to apply 
 
If you are looking to make a meaningful impact and join our team, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your updated CV online to be considered for this role.   
 
If you have any questions or issues submitting your application, please reach out to careers@dtinfrastructure.com.au.

Why You Should Apply
This is a career-defining FP&A role inside one of Australia's most significant ASX-listed resources businesses. If you are a commercially sharp finance professional who has built strong foundations in FP&A or management accounting and you are ready to step into a role that genuinely stretches you, this is it. You will partner directly with site leadership and senior operational decision makers, providing the financial insight that drives cost discipline, capital efficiency and strategic outcomes across a business operating at real scale. The finance function here is well-resourced, high performing and led by people who understand what good looks like. If you thrive in environments where your work actually matters and your analysis is used, not just filed, you will accelerate here faster than anywhere else in the Brisbane market.

The Opportunity
Reporting into a structured and high performing corporate finance team, the Senior FP&A Analyst is a critical business partnering role based in Brisbane's CBD. You will lead end to end budgeting and forecasting cycles, maintain long-range operational models and deliver performance reporting that is genuinely used by the people running the business. This is not a back office role. You will sit at the intersection of finance and operations, partnering with site leadership, operations teams and senior stakeholders to provide financial input into plans, improvement initiatives and capital decisions. The business operates across multiple sites and asset classes at significant scale, meaning the complexity, scope and quality of work here is genuinely differentiated from most commercial finance roles available in the market right now.

What You'll Do

Lead the annual budgeting process, rolling forecasts and long-range operational models across the business
Prepare monthly management reporting and cost performance analysis with clear, actionable commentary for senior stakeholders
Conduct variance analysis and proactively identify financial trends, risks and opportunities across the business
Develop and maintain KPI frameworks and financial dashboards used by operational and corporate leadership
Partner with site leadership and operations teams to provide financial input into plans, business cases and improvement initiatives
Support CAPEX planning, tracking and investment appraisals, monitoring project performance against approved budgets
Contribute to month end close processes and support cost control governance frameworks across the function
Who You Are and What You'll Bring

You are a commercially sharp finance professional with a genuine track record in FP&A, commercial finance or management accounting. You communicate with confidence, build strong relationships with non-finance stakeholders and understand that the value of analysis lies in the decisions it drives. You bring intellectual curiosity, a bias for action and the ability to operate with credibility at a senior level. You do not wait to be told what to do. You take ownership, move with purpose and hold yourself to a high standard.

CA or CPA qualified, or working towards, with a degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics or Commerce
5 or more years of experience in FP&A, commercial finance or management accounting
Strong financial modelling and forecasting capability with advanced Excel skills
Experience with Power BI or similar reporting and visualisation tools is advantageous
Demonstrated ability to engage and influence senior stakeholders across finance and operations
Experience in mining, resources or asset-intensive industries is preferred but not essential
The Offer

This role is based in the CBD with flexible start and finish times, sitting within a high performing and well-resourced finance function with genuine career depth. It is particularly well suited to someone with mine site experience who is looking to transition into a corporate office environment, partnering with senior leadership and internal departments across BAU finance, improvement initiatives and governance projects where your analysis genuinely shapes decisions.

About the Organisation

This organisation is a well-established operator within the Australian healthcare sector, currently undertaking a large-scale operational program as part of a broader period of change.

The work involves coordinating complex process changes across multiple hospital sites, with a strong focus on ensuring continuity of critical operational functions.

About the Role

You will support a complex program of work focused on separating and transitioning operational processes across multiple sites, ensuring they are clearly understood, documented and ready for handover.

Importantly, this is not a strategy or transformation design role, and it is not a data analyst position.

This role is about:

Getting into the detail
Working closely with SMEs
Mapping and documenting real-world processes
Supporting a structured and safe operational transition
What You’ll Be Doing
 
Process Mapping & Documentation

Engage with SMEs across central teams and site-based operations

Document “as-is” processes, including areas that are currently undocumented
Supporting the Program

Prepare clear, structured outputs for handover to stakeholders
Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with project leads, SMEs and operational teams
Gather information, validate outputs and follow up on actions
Present findings and documentation back to stakeholders
Execution & Coordination

Work within an existing project plan with multiple workstreams and tasks
Assist with tracking deliverables and ensuring timelines are met
Support broader program activity as required
Testing Support (Light Touch)

Assist with UAT activities where system or process changes occur
What This Role Is (And Isn’t)

This role IS:

A hands-on Business Analyst role
Focused on process mapping, documentation and delivery
A practical, “get it done” role in a fast-moving environment
This role is NOT:

A data / reporting or Power BI role
A pure strategy or process improvement role
A senior architect-level position owning the full program
What Experience You’ll Need

Proven experience as a Business Analyst in process-driven environments
Strong capability in:
Process mapping
Documentation
Stakeholder engagement
Experience working in:
Complex or fast-paced environments
Operational or transformation programs
Exposure to healthcare, billing or operational processes is advantageous
Strong working knowledge of Excel, Visio (or similar) and documentation tools
What Will Set You Apart

Ability to extract information from SMEs and translate into clear outputs
Strong organisation and follow-through
Comfortable working in ambiguity
A pragmatic, hands-on mindset
What’s In It For You

This is a high-impact contract role offering exposure to a complex and business-critical healthcare program.

You’ll work closely with experienced stakeholders and play a key role in ensuring critical processes are successfully transitioned and operational from day one.

Working as part of the Change and Adoption Team, the role focuses on understanding business impacts, supporting learning and communication activities, and ensuring stakeholders are prepared to transition to new systems, processes and ways of working.

Role

Learning and Capability Support
Support learning needs analysis activities and validate learning requirements arising from change impacts.

Requirements

Demonstrated experience supporting organisational change initiatives within a project or program environment.
Experience conducting change impact assessments and supporting business readiness activities.

If interested in this role please apply today or alternatively send your resume to Neema Mehra, neema.mehra@persolapac.com Please note that due to the high volume of applicants only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Role Highlights:
Exciting opportunity to join a high-performing tax team within a global organisation. This role offers exposure to both compliance and advisory, working closely with senior stakeholders while contributing to tax strategy, governance, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Reporting into senior tax leadership, this role will see you take ownership of a broad range of tax compliance obligations across corporate tax, GST, and FBT. On a regular basis, you will be responsible for preparing tax returns, conducting detailed analysis, and ensuring all reporting requirements are met accurately and on time. You will also work closely with external advisors and internal stakeholders to support ongoing tax matters.

Beyond compliance, you will contribute to advisory work by researching and preparing technical tax advice to support business decisions. You will play a key role in maintaining governance frameworks, identifying process improvements, and supporting the implementation of tax-related initiatives across the organisation.

Responsibilities
* Prepare and review corporate tax, GST, and FBT returns

About us

Perth Radiological Clinic (PRC) is Western Australia's largest and most established private radiology provider, proudly doctor-owned and serving the community for over 75 years.

With a team of over 130 doctors and 1,250 health professionals and support staff, we deliver high-quality diagnostic imaging across more than 20 hospital and community sites, powered by cutting-edge technology and a commitment to compassionate care.

About the role

We're looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join our Operations team in a part-time capacity. Based at our Subiaco office, and travelling to other sites as required, you will report to the Chief Operating Officer, with a dotted line to the CEO. This is a high-impact role for someone who knows how to turn complex data into decisions that drive real operational improvement.

Working closely with Finance and Operations leaders, you'll own the analytical function that underpins performance reporting, workforce productivity, risk oversight, and strategic business cases at executive and Board level. The arrangement is genuinely flexible — we're open to discussing FTE and working days with the right person.

Key responsibilities include:

Acting as Business Partner to the Finance and Operations teams, facilitating alignment between the two

Identifying trends in demand, utilisation, capacity, and turnaround times to support operational efficiency and service quality

Evaluating workforce productivity — roster efficiency, workload distribution, overtime, staffing mix — to support safe and sustainable operations

Designing and delivering executive and Board-level reporting, including monthly performance packs, KPI dashboards, and trend analysis

Developing and modelling business cases in Excel for Board approval, including preparation of Board papers

Supporting annual Operations budget development in partnership with Finance

Assisting in the development of Power BI dashboards to track performance and productivity

Maintaining the Operational risk register and developing a cadence of review to ensure risks are captured, managed, and mitigated

Undertaking ad hoc data analysis and insights for the CEO and COO

About you

You're a commercially minded analyst who's as comfortable in a Board paper as you are in a pivot table. You build trust with senior stakeholders quickly, communicate complexity clearly, and take ownership of your work without needing to be directed.

To be successful in this role, you will have:

Proven experience as a Business Partner to Finance and/or Operations functions, supporting decision-making, performance improvement, and cost management

Strong commercial and financial acumen — able to analyse revenue, expenditure, productivity, and growth drivers

The ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for executive and Board audiences

Advanced Excel skills, including business case modelling

Experience designing KPI dashboards, monthly performance packs, and trend analysis reporting

Experience assisting with or developing Power BI dashboards

Desirable: experience in workforce productivity analysis (roster efficiency, utilisation, staffing mix), operational risk registers, and/or budget development in a complex services environment

Job Title: Senior Data Enablement Analyst-Medicare Mental Health Check In
Ad Text:
Real Impact at Scale -Your work will directly influence clinical and operational decision-making across a national health system - improving patient outcomes, service delivery, and performance at scale.
Own and Shape the Analytics Function-Move beyond reporting into true enablement - acting as a product owner for analytics assets, shaping KPI frameworks, and embedding data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
Work at the Intersection of Data, Strategy and Healthcare Transformation-Engage with senior stakeholders, influence strategy, and contribute to system-wide transformation within one of Australia's largest and most respected health organisations.


What if your next role helped transform how data drives care across Australia?
St Vincent's Health Australia is building a future where data is not just reported - but actively shapes decisions, improves patient outcomes, and transforms how care is delivered.

We are now seeking a Senior Enablement Analyst to play a critical role in enabling data-driven decision-making across one of Australia's largest and most complex health organisations.

This is a rare opportunity to move beyond traditional reporting and step into a role where your insights directly influence clinical excellence, operational performance, and system-wide transformation.


About the Program - Medicare Mental Health Check-In
St Vincent's Health Australia has partnered with the Australian Government to deliver the Medicare Mental Health Check-In - a national digital mental health service designed to help Australians access care earlier and more easily.

This program brings together multiple national partners across technology, clinical workforce, digital innovation and lived experience to deliver a new model of accessible, scalable mental health support.

As part of this broader transformation, data and analytics play a critical role in enabling service performance, improving outcomes, and ensuring the program delivers meaningful impact at scale.


The Opportunity
As a Senior Enablement Analyst, you will sit at the intersection of business and technology - acting as the critical bridge between data, systems, and decision-making.

You will partner with clinical, operational, and corporate teams to bring data to life - translating complex information into meaningful insights that shape how performance is understood, measured, and improved.

Your work will ensure leaders have the clarity and confidence to make informed decisions that enhance care, improve efficiency, and support innovative, digitally enabled services - including national mental health initiatives.

This isn't just a reporting role - it's about storytelling, influence, and embedding a culture where data drives better outcomes.

Senior Change Specialist - 6 month contract



Your New Company
You will be part of a large, complex global organisation undertaking a significant transformation of its project delivery capability. The program is implementing a new global project delivery system across three economic regions, supporting approximately 8,500 users worldwide and involving the migration of around 70,000 active projects.
In addition to the core system implementation, the broader program includes multiple initiatives such as enhanced project delivery learning, rostering improvements, and updates to enterprise‑wide standards, processes and ways of working. This is a fast‑paced, high‑impact transformation delivering lasting change across the organisation's global portfolio.


Your New Role
As Senior Change Specialist, you will play a key role in driving change and adoption activities for the rollout of the new project delivery system. Reporting to the Program Change Manager, you will focus on delivering tactical, practical change interventions that ensure stakeholders are informed, prepared and supported throughout each implementation release.
You will work closely with cross‑functional teams including learning, communications, content management and support teams to translate change impacts into actionable materials and ensure a smooth transition for end users before, during and after go‑live.


What You'll Need to Succeed
To be successful in this role, you will bring strong experience supporting large‑scale system implementations in complex, fast‑moving environments. You will demonstrate:

Proven experience in change management for large system or digital transformation programs, ideally within global or multi‑region environments
Strong capability in conducting and maintaining change impact assessments and translating impacts into clear actions
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to tailor messaging to different audiences
Experience working across cross‑functional delivery teams and supporting release‑based implementations
Familiarity with structured change methodologies such as Prosci / ADKAR (or equivalent)
Ability to create clear, concise communications and high‑quality visual presentations aligned to brand and corporate standards
Experience in consulting or advisory environments (highly advantageous)
Strong organisational skills and the ability to operate effectively in ambiguous, fast‑changing contexts
A proactive, solutions‑focused mindset with strong influencing and collaboration skills
What You'll Get in Return
In return, you will have the opportunity to contribute to a high‑profile global transformation program with meaningful business impact. You'll work alongside experienced change, delivery and leadership professionals in a collaborative and supportive environment, with exposure to large‑scale system implementation and enterprise change.


This is a 6‑month contract with a hybrid working model (three days per week in the CBD office and two days working from home). Some flexibility in working hours may be required to support stakeholders across different global time zones.


This role offers a flexible hybrid working model, strong visibility across the program, and the chance to apply your change expertise in a complex, global setting while delivering tangible outcomes for thousands of users worldwide.





What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

About us

Boeing Defence Australia (BDA) is shaping the future of aerospace and delivering some of the Australian Defence Force's most important capabilities and programs. Joining BDA is a chance to make your mark, working with a diverse team that is united in pushing the boundaries of imagination and excellence. We currently employ 3,200 people across 14 sites in Australia and three international locations and have a range of opportunities available as we plan for future growth.

The Opportunity

As part of BDA the Business Operations team, the Data Governance Lead will be responsible for establishing and maturing the BDA-wide data governance program. This position will be instrumental in fostering a culture of data quality, efficiency and security across all levels of the organization. The Data Governance Lead will be responsible for managing and maintaining the data governance framework, facilitating governance forums, and overseeing monitoring, reporting and continuous improvement of data governance activities and processes. This role involves developing, nurturing, and leveraging cross- business relationships to maximise business value of data governance.

Key Responsibilities:
Lead deﬁning and reﬁning of BDA's data governance framework.
Establish, maintain and continuously improve BDA-level data governance policies, standards, procedures and frameworks.
Lead integration and alignment between data governance & business and technical disciplines, including architecture.
Facilitate the recognition and engagement of data owners and data stewards across different departments, acknowledging their existing roles in data management.
Lead and manage data governance forums, workshops and training sessions, ensuring participation and promoting data governance literacy across the organization.
Coordinate and monitor progress of the data strategy implementation.
Lead the coordination of and provide advice on data risks, issues and escalated matters.
Monitor data governance implementation progress and periodically assess BDA data maturity and capability uplift while overseeing data governance reporting, monitoring and continuous improvement.
Promote and educate key stakeholders about the data governance program, including key priorities and beneﬁts.
Required Qualiﬁcations, Skills & Experience
Minimum 5 years' experience in data governance, data management, data quality or related roles
Strong understanding of data governance concepts: metadata management, data lineage, data quality, master data management, data privacy and data lifecycle management.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science or related ﬁeld.
Experience with governance enabling technologies and tools (data catalog, metadata repository, data quality tools, MDM platforms) and ability to deﬁne tool requirements and vendor selection criteria.
Excellent stakeholder management and inﬂuencing skills; ability to work across senior business, legal/compliance, and IT audiences.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience deﬁning governance metrics and KPIs.
Clear written and verbal communication skills; experience creating policies, playbooks, training material and executive presentations.
Desirable Qualiﬁcations & Experience
Certiﬁcations such as CDMP (Certiﬁed Data Management Professional), DGSP (Data Governance and Stewardship Professional) or relevant data privacy certiﬁcations.
Practical experience implementing and operating data governance programs across business and IT stakeholders.
Change management or organizational design experience.
Experience in regulated industries (ﬁnance, healthcare, aerospace/defense) or large-scale enterprise environments.
Working conditions and travel
Hybrid work model with 3 days presence in the office.
Occasional travel to business sites and vendor locations is required.

Beneﬁts

Job Title: Group Procurement Operations, Systems & Analytics Manager
Ad Text:
The SunRice Group is an Australian ASX-listed branded food group, comprising multiple businesses, assets and operations across Australia, New Zealand, the Middle East, the United States, The Pacific Islands and Asia.  SunRice was built by the Australian rice industry over many decades and today is one of Australia’s leading branded food exporters, with approximately 2,000 employees and operations in 11 countries.

SunRice is a great place to work, providing a real opportunity for passionate people to make a difference.

 

About the role

We are looking for an experienced Procurement Operations, Systems & Analytics Manager to play a critical role in transforming a developing, global procurement function into a best‑in‑class operation.

This is a high‑impact role where you will help uplift procurement maturity by embedding robust processes, improving efficiencies, leveraging data and automation, and enabling the category management team to deliver maximum value through tendering and supplier strategies.

Reporting to the Group Procurement Director, you will lead procurement analytics, systems and operational enablement across a large, complex spend portfolio. This role is pivotal in moving procurement forward on their transformation journey - supporting stronger decision‑making, governance, sustainability outcomes, and financial performance across the business.

If you enjoy building, improving and modernising procurement functions - and know what “good” looks like in a mature environment - this role offers genuine scope to make a lasting impact.

 

Key responsibilities will include:

Driving the transformation and standardisation of procurement processes, tools and governance across global operations

Improving procurement efficiency and effectiveness through automation, better data quality and smarter use of systems

Developing and maintaining spend analytics, dashboards and reporting to support executive, board and finance requirements

Providing hands‑on operational support to category managers, including tender analysis, total cost modelling and sourcing insights to achieve best value outcomes

Enabling the category teams with clear data, tools and insights to improve sourcing decisions across contracts

Leading improvements across the source‑to‑pay lifecycle, including spend analytics, contract management and supplier performance reporting

Supporting the implementation and optimisation of procurement technologies and digital tools

Delivering procurement pipeline, savings, ROI and KPI reporting across the global function

Partnering with sustainability and ESG teams to support sustainable procurement reporting, including modern slavery and ESG requirements 

 

About You 

You are a commercially minded procurement professional who thrives in environments where there is opportunity to build, uplift and transform.

You bring experience from a more mature procurement function and can pragmatically apply best practice to a setting that is still evolving. You are confident working with data, comfortable influencing senior stakeholders, and motivated by the challenge of improving how things are done.

 

You will bring:

5–7+ years’ experience in procurement operations, analytics or enablement, ideally within a mature procurement function

Strong understanding of end‑to‑end procurement and tendering, with the ability to support category teams to deliver best‑value outcomes

Proven experience improving procurement processes, efficiencies and governance, including the use of data, automation and digital tools

Advanced analytical capability, with confidence creating dashboards, insights and executive‑ready reporting

Experience working with ERP, BI and e‑procurement platforms

Ability to clearly present insights and influence stakeholders across a global business

Exposure to sustainable procurement, ESG or supply chain sustainability reporting (beneficial)

A hands‑on, collaborative mindset and a genuine passion for transforming and uplifting procurement capability

 

What’s in it for you

At SunRice Group, we believe great work starts with feeling supported, valued and inspired. When you join our team, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, growth and everyday life.

SunRice Product Allowance: Take home a taste of what we do best. You’ll receive a weekly allowance of SunRice Group products, so you can enjoy and share the food we’re proud to make.

Leave: Enjoy competitive leave benefits, including volunteer leave and the option to purchase additional leave when you need extra flexibility.

Wellness Reimbursement: Your wellbeing matters. Twice a year, you’ll be reimbursed for fitness memberships or other health and wellbeing initiatives that help you feel your best

 

All correspondence will be dealt with in strict confidence.

SunRice is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. SunRice will consider qualified applicants for employment based on their merit, individual skills, qualifications and other appropriate criteria relevant to the role for which they are applying.

A leading Australian multi-division group is undergoing a major finance transformation, building a modern, insight-led Commercial Finance function. This is a rare opportunity to step into a true build role, shaping Group FP&A, planning systems, and performance insight from the ground up. 

The role

The FP&A Manager will act as the central planning engine for the Group, owning budgeting, forecasting and long-range planning across a complex multi-entity environment. You will consolidate divisional inputs into a single Group view, ensuring consistency in assumptions, governance, and financial targets, while delivering high-quality insights to support executive and Board-level decision-making.

A key focus of the role is leading the transition from Excel-based planning to a system-enabled, driver-based planning environment, including implementation of a new Group planning and forecasting tool. You will partner closely with Finance Business Partners embedded across the business, who focus on commercial support, while FP&A sets frameworks, standards and Group performance visibility.

Key responsibilities include leading annual budgets and rolling forecasts, developing driver-based financial models, delivering executive reporting and variance analysis, establishing planning governance and cadence and supporting strategic modelling, scenario analysis and investment decisions. You will also lead implementation of the Group planning system, driving adoption, training, and change management across Finance and the broader business.

About you:

you are an experienced FP&A or Group Finance professional with strong budgeting, forecasting, and financial modelling capability. You will have exposure to finance transformation or planning system implementation, strong stakeholder engagement skills, and experience working in complex multi-entity or divisional organisations.
This is a foundational role where you will define and build Group FP&A capability, lead a major systems and process transformation, and help shift the finance function from reporting-focused to insight-led, commercially driven decision support.

Why This Role

This is a foundational FP&A leadership role where you will:

Build and define Group planning capability from the ground up
Lead a major finance systems and process transformation
Influence executive decision-making and Group strategy
Move the finance function from reporting-focused to insight-led
Play a key role in shaping a scalable, modern Commercial Finance function

Job Title: Senior Manager Employer Governance and Reporting
Ad Text:
About WorkCover Queensland

For over 20 years, WorkCover Queensland has been a self-funded, government-owned leader in the state’s workforce, providing empathetic and efficient workers’ compensation insurance. We are currently embarking on an enterprise-wide transformation guided by our 2030 Strategy. This is an exciting chapter focused on keeping Queenslanders working through innovation, integrity, and high-quality service delivery.

They are seeking a Senior Manager, Employer Governance & Reporting to play a pivotal role in enabling our Employer Group strategy through best-in-class data, governance and reporting capability.

About the Opportunity

Reporting to the GM Employer and sitting within the Employer Group Leadership Team, this role is central to ensuring the Account Management function is set up for success - with the right data, systems, processes and reporting frameworks in place.

This is a high-impact, build-and-shape role. You will co-design elements of the employer strategy while leading key initiatives such as CRM implementation, analytics uplift and the development of account management frameworks.

While there are no direct reports initially, there is clear scope to build and lead a team over time.
 
Key Responsibilities

Strategy, Governance & Enablement

Enable the Employer Group strategy and Engagement Plan through robust governance and reporting frameworks;
Co-design employer strategy, segmentation and account management frameworks; and
Lead governance processes, including incident management and audit support.
Data, Reporting & Insights

Establish trusted data, dashboards and reporting to drive performance and decision-making;
Deliver meaningful insights on employer trends, risks and opportunities; and
Lead the reporting cadence across the Employer Group.
Systems & Transformation

Own and lead CRM implementation and optimisation;
Partner with Technology to uplift systems, integrations and data capability; and
Drive analytics capability, including dashboarding (e.g. Power BI).
Capability & Stakeholder Engagement

Build governance and reporting capability through coaching, training and mentoring;
Partner closely with senior stakeholders, including the GM and ELT, providing clear and actionable insights; and
Support the Account Management function to lift performance through data-driven approaches.
About You

You’re a commercially minded and strategically focused leader who enjoys operating in greenfield or evolving environments, where you can build, influence and deliver tangible outcomes.

You will bring:

Strong experience in data governance, reporting and analytics leadership;
Proven capability in CRM development and/or optimisation;
Experience designing and governing enterprise-wide frameworks with commercial rigour;
Exposure to audit processes, governance and senior stakeholder briefing;
The ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights;
Experience in workers’ compensation is highly regarded; however, backgrounds in insurance or financial services (e.g. superannuation) will also be considered; and
Exposure to Power BI and experience building capability in others will be advantageous.

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

 

What you will bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Manager, Customer Insights

Clerk Grade: 11/12

Annual Salary Range: $149,739 - $173,174 plus superannuation

Employment Type: Ongoing, Full-time

Location: Sydney, McKell Building

About the team

In Customer Insights & Experience (CIE), our focus is ensuring the voices of people, communities, and businesses sit at the centre of decision-making and service delivery across NSW Government.

We help government understand what communities need, think, and experience - from detailed service interactions to broader community sentiment - and turn those insights into better decisions and better services.

You'll join a passionate team of specialists, with expertise in research, insights, customer experience, and service design. Together, we deliver actionable insights and practical improvements that shape policy, investment, communications, and service delivery across the whole of NSW Government - including departments, agencies, Ministers and Cabinet.

We generate actionable insights. Our insights specialists provide a deep understanding of the people, communities, and businesses to inform government decisions and identify opportunities to improve outcomes.
We turn insights into action. Our customer experience and service design specialists apply human-centered design, co-design, usability testing, and service transformation methods to create, refine, and uplift service experiences, ensuring tangible improvements for the people of NSW.

Your day-to-day

As a Manager, Customer Insights, you will play a lead role in the design, execution, and delivery of key research and insights projects - managing a team of research and insights specialists and working with key stakeholders to deliver actionable insights that inform government decision-making and service delivery.

This role requires a leader who can combine technical research expertise with strategic thinking, stakeholder engagement and team development. You will guide your team through rigorous research design, fieldwork, data analysis, insights development and presentations, ensuring insights are simple, compelling, and actionable.

In this role, you will:

Lead and manage a team of insights specialists, providing coaching and professional development to uplift capability and ensure consistent delivery of high-quality insights.
Oversee a portfolio of concurrent research and insights projects - including BAU reporting, whole-of-government insights programs, and bespoke research addressing agency-specific needs.
Proactively identify, leverage and integrate numerous datasets to develop insights reports with simple, clear, and actionable insights to stakeholders across all levels of government.
Engage with stakeholders across NSW Government to understand challenges, clarify objectives, scope work and translate needs into well-designed, costed insights proposals.
Lead the design, delivery, and execution of ad-hoc quantitative and qualitative insights projects to address key objectives and inform government decision-making, including research design, questionnaire design, discussion guide development, fieldwork or supplier management, data analysis, insights reporting, and presentations.
Deliver clear, actionable insights through reports, dashboards, presentations and workshops that support evidence-based decisions across policy, investment, communications, and service design and delivery.

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Extensive experience leading complex research and insights projects end-to-end, across qualitative and quantitative methods, sampling design, fieldwork, data analysis and insights communication.
Strong consulting capabilities - able to engage with senior stakeholders, translate ambiguous or high-level questions into structured research briefs, and design methodological approaches that deliver clear and actionable insights.
A proven ability to synthesise complex data into meaningful, actionable insights and communicate them effectively through written reports, visualisations, presentations and facilitated workshops.
Strong people leadership experience, including coaching, performance management, capability uplift and fostering a culture of excellence in insights and storytelling.
Strong project and portfolio management skills, with the ability to balance multiple priorities, maintain high quality standards and meet deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
Deep understanding of customer insights, community sentiment and customer experience measurement programs - ideally within large, complex or public sector contexts.
Highly developed interpersonal and influencing skills, with the ability to build trust, manage expectations and partner effectively across diverse teams and executive levels.
Experience with Q Research Software.
Strong proficiency in the Microsoft suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word).

Company description:



About Newmont

Newmont is the world's leading gold company, producing gold as well as copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum across operations in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America and North America. In Australia, Newmont operates across Western Australia, the Northern Territory and New South Wales, supported by teams in Perth, Brisbane and Melbourne.

At Newmont, people create the value in the metals we mine and are ambassadors in the communities where they live and work. As a team, we foster an environment in support of our people vision, where everyone belongs, thrives and is valued. We have built a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can bring their differences to support delivering results together.

Join us and unearth your potential in a global company that is at the forefront of shaping the future of the mining industry.



Job description:



Your impact
Reporting to the Data Architecture Lead, you will support the development and evolution of Newmont's enterprise data platform by designing and delivering scalable data and AI solutions.

You will work with large, complex datasets to build and deploy modern data pipelines, machine learning and AI-enabled solutions that solve real business problems. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, data science and platform delivery, partnering closely with architecture, engineering and business teams to deliver secure, scalable and repeatable solutions.


As part of our team you will

Design, build and deploy data pipelines and AI/ML-enabled solutions using cloud-based platforms
Work with large structured and unstructured datasets to support enterprise data use cases
Translate solution design into executable technical delivery in an agile environment
Contribute to the standardisation of architecture and deployment patterns
Support the development of scalable, secure and repeatable data solutions
Apply and embed data governance, data quality and ethical AI practices
Collaborate with AI/ML, Digital Enablement and operational teams to solve business problems
Support end-to-end data workflows including ingestion, transformation and consumption
Optimise data processes and models for performance, scalability and reliability
Contribute to continuous improvement of the enterprise data platform and data capabilities


Profile description:



Prerequisites for your success in the role

Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
Strong SQL capability (essential), including experience working with large datasets
Experience with Python for data engineering or machine learning use cases
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Azure, AWS or similar)
Understanding of data governance, data quality and data management practices
Ability to translate business requirements into clear technical solutions
Experience working with enterprise-scale data or complex data environments

Well regarded:

Experience with Databricks or similar lakehouse platforms
Experience with large-scale data processing (e.g. Spark)
Exposure to machine learning frameworks (e.g. TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Experience with APIs and system integration in enterprise environments
Experience with GenAI, LLMs, or modern AI solution design (e.g. RAG, vector databases)
Experience in mining, resources, or heavy industry environments
Understanding of operational data and value chains

Your work environment
This role is ideally based in Perth, however flexible work arrangements, including remote options, will be considered in line with business and operational requirements.

You will work closely with a highly capable technical team across architecture, engineering and data functions, supporting stakeholders across operations and digital teams.

Occasional travel may be required to engage with stakeholders or support specific initiatives.

About us

Founded in 2020, the team at The Lookout Way set out to transform the delivery of in-home care by blending cutting-edge technology with deep industry insight. Born from firsthand experience with the challenges facing Aged Care providers, we built the only digital care management platform created by care, for care.

Our flagship platform, Lookout, is an all-in-one SaaS solution that empowers providers and their clients with the tools and visibility needed to deliver high-quality, profitable care. With a strong focus on intuitive design, it connects staff, care workers, and clients through personalised interfaces tailored to each user’s role.

About the Role

As our Project Delivery Lead, you won’t just be "managing tasks"—you will be the strategic architect behind the end-to-end implementation of the Lookout Care Suite for our largest, most complex customers. You will bridge the gap between technical system architecture and executive-level business goals, ensuring that our partners in the Aged Care and NDIS sectors aren’t just installing software, but undergoing a successful digital transformation.

This is a high-visibility role requiring a rare blend of technical depth and "C-suite ready" communication. Your mission is to lead cross-functional teams to deliver best-in-class customer experiences on time and within budget.

The core responsibilities of the role include:

Define scope, goals, and deliverables for large-scale implementations, managing detailed project plans and budgets.
Establish deep-rooted relationships with executive leadership and boards, providing high-level governance reporting and proactive risk mitigation.
Become an expert in the Lookout product ecosystem. You’ll translate complex system architecture (APIs, BI tools, and integrations) into sequenced, actionable deliverables.
Facilitate high-impact workshops and discovery sessions to identify opportunities for operational uplift and value activation.

Do you have what it takes?

You are a big picture thinker who never loses sight of the details. You understand that successful system onboarding is 20% technology and 80% people and process, and you thrive in environments where change needs to be practical, scalable, and well‑managed across the organisation.  

5+ years’ experience delivering software implementations within mid‑sized organisations (e.g. SaaS vendors, agencies) leading projects that balance enterprise‑grade rigor with real‑world operational constraints. 
Strong understanding of system design and the SDLC, with hands‑on experience integrating HRIS, Finance, and Payroll systems in organisations where systems, teams, and budgets must work closely together. 
Experience in Aged Care and/or NDIS environments is highly regarded, particularly within providers where regulatory compliance, rostering, clinical delivery, and financial processes intersect daily.  
Highly developed emotional intelligence, with the ability to translate technical concepts into clear, practical language for non‑technical stakeholders across diverse teams.  
Proven experience managing and delivering projects within defined timeframes, working with cross functional teams where adaptability and collaboration are critical to success. 
Ability to obtain and maintain clear, relevant criminal history checks.  
Unlimited working rights in Australia. 

What's in it for you?

The way we work and the perks we offer are designed to help you live a fulfilling life. The work we do is meaningful, and the problems we solve are interesting.

A competitive salary package that will recognise your skills and experience.
You’ll work remotely from anywhere in Australia as long as you have a stable internet connection and a comfortable setup. We do have offices throughout the country for face to face contact.
Annual $500 health and well-being allowance to spend on anything contributing to your health and well-being away from work.
On top of the standard 4 weeks of annual leave, after your second year, you will accumulate an additional day of annual leave each year you are with us. You will also have access to our Pandemic Leave policy and have the chance to purchase additional annual leave.
Access to our employee assistance programme, where you can receive free confidential and professional counselling.
Parental leave benefits –All caregivers who are eligible for the government’s paid parental leave scheme will receive top-up wage payments. Additionally, primary caregivers will receive top-up superannuation payments for their first year of unpaid parental leave.
Extend the Five Good Friends care to a loved one at a special rate. The nominated family member will receive Care Managed services at Self-Managed prices (values vary depending on circumstances).
Working for a company that has an employee engagement score that is 17% higher than the industry average, and 7% higher than the top 25% industry average (we are actually a great place to work).
And… after 5 years of continuous service, you will receive a trip to Okinawa, Japan - the community that inspired the creation of Five Good Friends. Costs of your return economy airfare, 5 nights in hotel accommodation and airport transfers will be paid.

https://www.thelookoutway.com

Manager, Customer Insights (Have Your Say)

Clerk Grade: 11/12

Annual Salary Range: $149,739 - $173,174 plus superannuation

Employment Type: Ongoing, Full-time

Location: Sydney, McKell Building

About the team

In Customer Insights & Experience (CIE), our focus is ensuring the voices of people, communities, and businesses sit at the centre of decision-making and service delivery across NSW Government.

We help government understand what communities need, think, and experience - from detailed service interactions to broader community sentiment - and turn those insights into better decisions and better services.

You'll join a passionate team of specialists, with expertise in research, insights, customer experience, and service design. Together, we deliver actionable insights and practical improvements that shape policy, investment, communications, and service delivery across the whole of NSW Government - including departments, agencies, Ministers and Cabinet.

We generate actionable insights. Our insights specialists provide a deep understanding of the people, communities, and businesses to inform government decisions and identify opportunities to improve outcomes.
We turn insights into action. Our customer experience and service design specialists apply human-centered design, co-design, usability testing, and service transformation methods to create, refine, and uplift service experiences, ensuring tangible improvements for the people of NSW.

About Have Your Say

Have Your Say is NSW Government's central hub for public consultation, where communities can share their views on government projects, policies, services and reforms. It provides a single, statewide listing of all public consultations currently open for comment, ensuring transparency and enabling people to easily find and participate in engagement opportunities.

Your day-to-day

As the Manager, Customer Insights (Have Your Say), you will lead a team responsible for designing, executing and reporting on major community consultation research projects. You will ensure that insights from the public - including diverse voices across regional, urban, vulnerable and culturally diverse communities - directly inform NSW Government decision-making.

You will drive the end-to-end delivery of consultation research, working closely with key stakeholders and specialised teams to ensure research is robust, inclusive, accessible and aligned with NSW Government standards. This includes strong expertise in community consultation methodologies, questionnaire design, fieldwork management, data analysis, insights reporting and stakeholder engagement.

You will act as a strategic advisor across NSW Government, helping agencies scope consultation needs, design methodological approaches and deliver insights that shape policy, legislation, reform programs and service improvements.

In this role, you will:

Lead, coach and develop a team of insights specialists delivering high-quality community consultation research across the Have Your Say program.
Design, manage and deliver major consultation studies, including methodology development, survey and questionnaire design, sampling, recruitment, fieldwork, analysis, and insights reporting.
Collaborate with key internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure consultations are delivered to a high-standard, address key objectives, whilst meeting accessibility, inclusivity and governance standards.
Produce compelling, actionable insights through reports, dashboards, executive presentations and interactive workshops that influence decision-making.
Guide agencies on best‑practice consultation processes, including how to publish, promote and manage consultations on the Have Your Say platform.
Ensure privacy, data governance and ethical considerations are embedded in all consultation activities in line with NSW Government requirements.

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Extensive experience leading end-to-end research and community consultations, including questionnaire design, qualitative and quantitative methods, sampling, recruitment and fieldwork management.
Strong data analysis and storytelling capability - able to translate complex consultation data into meaningful and actionable insights for senior decision-makers.
Demonstrated experience managing multiple research projects concurrently, with high attention to quality, accuracy and timeliness.
Strong stakeholder engagement and consulting skills, including working with senior executives, policy leaders and communications teams to scope, design and deliver consultation programs.
Strong people leadership experience, including coaching, performance management, capability uplift and fostering a culture of excellence in insights and storytelling.
Strong understanding of NSW Government consultation requirements, and adherence to accessibility and plain‑English standards.
A commitment to community-centred decision-making and ensuring people across NSW have meaningful opportunities to participate in shaping government initiatives.
Experience with Q Research Software.
Strong proficiency in the Microsoft suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word).

What we need from you:

Please note candidates invited to interview will be required to complete additional assessments.

A talent pool may be created from this recruitment process to fill future ongoing and temporary opportunities.
